xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: Urjanidhi, 1, Barakhamba Lane, Connaught Place, New Delhi - 110 001
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: Urjanidhi'], 1, Barakhamba Lane, Connaught Place, New Delhi - 110 001
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfContactPerson: Shri Manish Kumar Agarwal Company Secretary
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesGeneralDisclosureExplanat: Essential Indicator 18)a) * PFC operates across India, covering 28 states and 8 UTs, and extends its resources by accessing both domestic and international capital markets. 20)a) *Other than permanent workers have been engaged to provide Support and Peripheral Services at PFC Ltd. through the outsourced agency. All compliance, payroll, and related administrative functions pertaining to this staff are managed by the said agency. 21) Board of Directors: In FY 2025-26 Q1, the Ministry of Power, Government of India appointed five Independent Directors, including one woman, starting April 17, 2025, and others on May 13 and 14, 2025. 22)The company has revised its methodology for calculating the turnover rate compared to the previous year. 25)* Details of Investors (including Bond Holders) /Shareholder are covered)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProductOrServiceSoldByTheEntit: Other Financial Services and Activities - Other Credit Granting
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan:
- PFC (Power Finance Corporation) specialises in offering interest-bearing loans to private sector borrowers and various Central and State utilities. - The organisation's client base includes central power utilities, state power utilities, private sector power utilities (including independent power producers), and joint sector power utilities. - PFC also provides financial services to power equipment manufacturers and companies involved in infrastructure and logistics.
PFC offers a variety of financial products, including long-term, medium-term, and short-term loans. These products are tailored to meet the unique needs of the power industry, supporting the entire value chain. This includes funding for new projects, upgrades, and expansions. The company's clientele also encompasses state governments, independent power producers, and both state and central utilities, playing a vital role in developing and sustaining the country's power infrastructure.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheHoldingOrSubsidiaryAs: Bhadla and Bikaner Complex Transmission Limited
NES Pune East New Transmission Limited
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksStakeHolderGroupFromWho: The complaint received on 29 March 2025 was disposed of on 8 April 2025.
Complaints received during the year were subsequently resolved by April 5, 2024
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForGrievanceRedressPoli: The Company has a dedicated grievance handling policy accessible to employees on the intranet. It features a three-tier system allowing permanent staff to escalate their issues through various levels. At Stage I, grievances are typically resolved within a week. If not satisfactorily addressed, the issue advances to Stage II, where it is resolved within 10 days of a written appeal. Should the employee remain unsatisfied after Stage II, they can appeal to a higher level. A decision at Stage III is made within one month of receiving the written appeal.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Risk - The threat of non-compliance puts the organisation at risk of incurring legal penalties and financial losses due to its inability to follow industry laws and regulations. Failing to comply with these laws would have a direct impact on the company's revenue and valuation, potentially resulting in a tarnished reputation and lost business opportunities. More compliant companies typically experience enhanced performance and greater efficiency in their processes. Compliance offers reassurance and provides investors with a more comprehensive understanding.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Please refer to the Governance Section in the latest ESG Report for FY 25
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: The company's incorporation of environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors into its lending practices presents an opportunity to promote eco-friendly activities and fund sustainable projects, thereby encouraging positive change and supporting long-term growth.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Please refer to the Governance Section in the latest ESG Report for FY 25
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: The risk of a data breach poses a threat to the exposure of our customers' personally identifiable information, which can harm our business relationship. Such a breach could compromise the confidentiality, integrity, and security of sensitive customer data, potentially eroding trust and damaging our reputation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Please refer to the IT Strategy Section in the latest ESG Report for FY 25
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Adhering to business ethics highlights the company's commitment to ethical behaviour and integrity in all interactions. By emphasising ethical practices, the company seeks to retain current customers and attract new ones. Upholding these principles fosters trust, strengthens relationships, and solidifies the company's reputation as a responsible and reliable financial institution.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Please refer to the Corporate Governance Section in the latest ESG Report for FY 25
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Employee Health and Positive Work Environment
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: This boosts efficiency, reduces employee attrition, enhances capabilities and creativity within the company, and increases diversity, which in turn improves the customer experience.

Having effective ESG oversight at the board level will improve overall ESG performance and demonstrate the company’s dedication to responsible business practices within its growth strategy. Embedding ESG into business operations can enhance risk management, governance, accountability, and reporting, as well as inform decision-making. This integration enables the identification and management of risks and opportunities related to environmental and social impacts, while also fostering trust and transparency with investors and stakeholders.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Please refer to the ESG Governance Section in the latest ESG Report for FY 25
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfThePoliciesExplanator: CSR and Sustainability Policy : https://www.pfcindia.com/DocumentRepository/ ckfinder/files/CSR/CSR%20Policy.pdf Fair Practice Code: https://www.pfcindia.com/DocumentRepository/ckfinder/files/Statutory_Requirements/Codes_and_Policies/Fair_Practices_Code/2020-09-02%20Fair%20Practice%20Code%20of%20PFC%20updated%20.pdf Code of Business Conduct and Ethics https://www.pfcindia.com/Default/ ViewFile/?id=1472556128687_Code%20of%20 Conduct%2017042015.pdf&path=Page&Name=Code%20 of%20Conduct%20for%20Board%20of%20Directors%20 and%20Senior%20Management%20Personnel Anti-Fraud Policy https://www.pfcindia.com/Default/ ViewFile/?id=1471520577857_ANTI%20FRAUD%20 POLICY%2005_11_2011.pdf&path=Page&Name=Anti%20 Fraud%20Policy Whistle-Blower Policy: https://www.pfcindia.com/Default/ViewFile/?id=1490188785276_WBP.pdf&path=Page Policy on Related Party Transactions https://www.pfcindia.com/Default/ViewFile/?id=1561552784406_Final%20Policy%20on%20RPT%2017052019.pdf&path=Page Policy on Material Subsidiary https://www.pfcindia.com/Default/ Dividend Distribution Policy https://www.pfcindia.com/Default/ViewFile/?id=1546009180778_DividendDistribution.pdf&path=Page Code of Practices & Procedures for Fair Disclosure of Unpublished Price Sensitive Information and Conduct for regulating, monitoring & reporting of trading in the securities of Power Finance Corporation Limited https://pfcindia.com/Default/ViewFile/?id=1614952208955_Insider_Trading_Code_Amended05032021.pdf&path=Page Policy for Determination of Materiality of Events https://www.pfcindia.com/DocumentRepository/ckfinder/files/Statutory_Requirements/Codes_and_Policies/Policy_for_Determination_of_Materiality_of_Events/Final%20Policy%20for%20Determination%20of%20Materiality%20of%20Events17052019.pdf

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible: Please refer to the Chairman & Managing Director’s Message in the ESG Report FY 25.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT:
At PFC, the governance structure is anchored by the Board of Directors, which stands as the ultimate authority responsible for steering the company in alignment with its strategic objectives and upholding rigorous ethical standards. In this comprehensive framework, the Board Committee on Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) and Sustainable Development (SD) plays a crucial and dynamic role in shaping the company's initiatives in these essential domains.
This specialised committee is responsible for advising the Board on a diverse range of strategies related to CSR and SD. It emphasises the significance of social responsibility and environmental sustainability as integral components of the company's mission. Through careful analysis and thoughtful recommendations, the committee aims to develop impactful programs that not only contribute positively to society but also promote sustainable business practices. By focusing on these critical areas, the committee seeks to foster a culture of responsibility and innovation that permeates the organisation, ultimately benefiting both the community and the environment.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: 1. Awareness workshop on Yoga 2. Session on Introduction to ESG and Materiality Assessment

1. Awareness Workshop on Sexual Harassment of Women at the Workplace (POSH ACT, 2013) 2. Workshop on Good Governance Practices 3. Awareness Workshop on Yoga 4. CDA Rules of PFC Workshop 5. CSR for High-Impact Corporate Performance 6. Environmental Regulations Facilitating ESG & Sustainability for Business 7. Workshop on Ethics & Governance 8. Yoga Workshop 9. Informative Session on ESG 10. Workshop on RTI Act 11. Workshop on Brain Yoga
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheRegulatoryOrEnforceme: BSE Limited & National Stock Exchange of India Limited
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:BriefOfTheMonetaryCaseForPenal: Penal actions prescribed for non-compliance with Applicable Regulation of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, Regulation 17(1), 17(2A), 18(1), 19(1)/19(2), 20(2)/(2A), 21(2).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol:
To ensure fair dealing and transparency in its lending operations, the Company has established a comprehensive Fair Practices Code (FPC) that aligns with the principles set forth by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). This code serves as a foundational guideline for ethical conduct, emphasising integrity in all business interactions.
The FPC is complemented by a detailed Code of Business Conduct & Ethics, designed explicitly for Board Members and Senior Management. This document outlines the responsibilities and ethical standards expected of those at the highest levels of the organisation. Additionally, the Company has implemented an Anti-Fraud Policy, which outlines proactive measures to prevent and detect fraud, creating a secure environment for its operations and stakeholders.
To further promote accountability, the Conduct, Discipline, and Appeal (CDA) Rules act as a framework for all employees, including the Board of Directors and Senior Management Personnel. These rules ensure strict adherence to relevant anti-bribery laws, rules, and regulations. The Company maintains a strict zero-tolerance policy towards any form of bribery or corruption. It is deeply committed to conducting its business with professionalism, fairness, and unwavering integrity, reinforcing its dedication to ethical practices in every aspect of its operations
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheEntityHaveProcesse:
Yes. The company has established a comprehensive code of business conduct and ethics that addresses a wide range of essential topics, including the management of conflicts of interest that may arise for both board members and senior management. To effectively navigate these conflicts, a well-defined procedure has been implemented that enables Directors to abstain from discussions or decisions where a conflict of interest may exist. Directors need to perform their duties with integrity, ensuring that their actions align with the best interests of the company. They are entrusted with the responsibility of operating free from external influences that could compromise their ability to make independent and impartial decisions, which are critical to fostering the success and growth of the Company.
Additionally, the code explicitly prohibits Directors from exploiting their positions for personal gain, whether directly or indirectly, at the expense of the Company's interests. To uphold these standards, both the Board of Directors and senior management are required to submit an annual declaration, affirming their commitment to abiding by the Code of Conduct. This declaration also includes a confirmation of their involvement in other companies, ensuring transparency and accountability in their professional engagements.
The Policy is available at https://www.pfcindia.com/Home/VS/63
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple1ExplanatoryText: Essential Indicator 3) *On receipt of letter/e-mail from stock exchanges imposing fine on PFC, the Company requests the exchange to withdraw their letters (imposing fine) as PFC is a CPSE under the administrative control of MOP, Government of India and the Directors on the Board of PFC are appointed by President of India, through Ministry of Power, Government of India. 8)Considering the nature of the entity's business, the said ratio is not very significant, as the cost of goods /services procured is negligible.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR:
Yes. The Company has a dedicated grievance handling policy available on the intranet. It features a 3-tier system that lets all permanent employees escalate concerns at multiple levels. At Stage I, grievances are addressed within a week. If not satisfactorily resolved, the issue is escalated to Stage II, where it is settled within 10 days of written notice. Unsatisfied with this outcome, employees can appeal to a higher level, with decisions made at Stage III within one month of receiving the written appeal.
The company also operates a Whistle-blower Initiative (WI), providing a formal platform for reporting various grievances. Employees receive information about the grievance process and WI through a dedicated module, and new hires are introduced to the WI during induction. The company has a policy in place to prevent and address sexual harassment, with an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) that is compliant with the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace Act, 2013. The ICC members are listed on the PFC notice board, and staff are regularly trained and made aware of sexual harassment prevention through ongoing programs.

Yes. The Company has a dedicated grievance handling policy available on the intranet. It features a 3-tier system that lets all permanent employees escalate concerns at multiple levels. At Stage I, grievances are addressed within a week. If not satisfactorily resolved, the issue is escalated to Stage II, where it is settled within 10 days of written notice. Unsatisfied with this outcome, employees can appeal to a higher level, with decisions made at Stage III within one month of receiving the written appeal.
The company also operates a Whistle-blower Initiative (WI), providing a formal platform for reporting various grievances. Employees receive information about the grievance process and WI through a dedicated module, and new hires are introduced to the WI during induction. The company has a policy in place to prevent and address sexual harassment, with an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) that is compliant with the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace Act, 2013. The ICC members are listed on the PFC notice board, and staff are regularly trained and made aware of sexual harassment prevention through ongoing programs.

Yes. The Company has a dedicated grievance handling policy available on the intranet. It features a 3-tier system that lets all permanent employees escalate concerns at multiple levels. At Stage I, grievances are addressed within a week. If not satisfactorily resolved, the issue is escalated to Stage II, where it is settled within 10 days of written notice. Unsatisfied with this outcome, employees can appeal to a higher level, with decisions made at Stage III within one month of receiving the written appeal.
The company also operates a Whistle-blower Initiative (WI), providing a formal platform for reporting various grievances. Employees receive information about the grievance process and WI through a dedicated module, and new hires are introduced to the WI during induction. The company has a policy in place to prevent and address sexual harassment, with an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) that is compliant with the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace Act, 2013. The ICC members are listed on the PFC notice board, and staff are regularly trained and made aware of sexual harassment prevention through ongoing programs.

Yes. The Company has a dedicated grievance handling policy available on the intranet. It features a 3-tier system that lets all permanent employees escalate concerns at multiple levels. At Stage I, grievances are addressed within a week. If not satisfactorily resolved, the issue is escalated to Stage II, where it is settled within 10 days of written notice. Unsatisfied with this outcome, employees can appeal to a higher level, with decisions made at Stage III within one month of receiving the written appeal.
The company also operates a Whistle-blower Initiative (WI), providing a formal platform for reporting various grievances. Employees receive information about the grievance process and WI through a dedicated module, and new hires are introduced to the WI during induction. The company has a policy in place to prevent and address sexual harassment, with an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) that is compliant with the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace Act, 2013. The ICC members are listed on the PFC notice board, and staff are regularly trained and made aware of sexual harassment prevention through ongoing programs.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd:
PFC is fully committed to fostering the physical and mental health and well-being of its employees through a variety of thoughtfully designed initiatives.
As part of our comprehensive Health and Safety program, several training workshops are scheduled for the fiscal year 2024-25, designed to enhance employee productivity and overall wellness. These initiatives include:
1. Workshop on Health & Stress Management: This interactive session will equip employees with practical strategies to manage stress effectively, creating an environment that boosts both mental resilience and productivity.
2. Two Workshops on Yoga: Participants will engage in this enlightening workshop designed to introduce the benefits of yoga and incorporate mindfulness practices into their daily routines.
3. Training for Internal Quality Auditors: A specialised program aimed at developing a skilled team of Internal Quality Auditors in alignment with ISO 9001:2015 and ISO 45001:2018 standards, ensuring a robust framework for quality and safety in our operations.
4. Workshop on ISO 45001:2018 - Occupational Health & Safety Standards: This session will delve into the essentials of occupational health and safety standards, empowering employees to recognise and implement these crucial practices in the workplace.
In addition to these workshops, the fiscal year 2024-25 witnessed the successful organisation of eight medical camps strategically located at the PFC Corporate Office, PFCCL, and regional offices in Mumbai and Chennai, providing accessible healthcare services to all employees. To further support holistic well-being, we offer daily virtual yoga sessions available to all employees and workers, fostering an inclusive and accessible approach to health and fitness.
At the Corporate Office, a dedicated doctor's room is maintained, staffed with experienced specialists across various fields, including Allopathic medicine, Ayurveda, Gynaecology, Homoeopathy, and Naturopathy. This ensures that employees have access to professional care during working hours, according to a set schedule. Moreover, to address urgent health issues, a Wellness Centre has been established at the Corporate Office. This facility is designed to handle emergencies and offer first aid, ensuring that employees have prompt access to medical assistance when needed. Through these initiatives, PFC demonstrates its unwavering commitment to the health and well-being of its workforce.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo:
PFC has established a comprehensive and meticulous process to identify health risks and hazards, ensuring a safe and healthy workplace for all employees. The company implements several critical measures, including:
- Annual Fire Training: Each year, employees participate in extensive fire training sessions designed to equip them with the knowledge and skills necessary to respond effectively in the event of a fire emergency.
- Yearly Evacuation Drills: To reinforce preparedness, PFC conducts evacuation drills annually, allowing employees to practice and become familiar with emergency procedures and exit routes.
- Regular Fire Safety Advisories: The company issues fire safety advisories to keep employees informed of the latest safety protocols and best practices, ensuring a safe working environment.
- Strategically Placed Fire Safety Signage: Clear and visible fire safety signs are installed at various points throughout the workplace, providing essential information and directions to enhance awareness and safety.
- E-surveillance and Alert Monitoring: Advanced electronic surveillance and alert monitoring systems are utilised to monitor the premises and promptly detect any potential hazards continuously.
- Comprehensive Fire and Burglar Alarm Systems: PFC is equipped with sophisticated fire and burglar alarm systems, featuring advanced fire detection technology and smoke sensors that provide prompt alerts in the event of emergencies.
- Readily Accessible Fire Extinguishers: Fire extinguishers are strategically placed throughout the facility, ensuring they are readily available to support firefighting efforts in the event of a fire.
Through these proactive measures, PFC demonstrates its commitment to maintaining a secure and health-conscious workplace for all its staff.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE: Please refer to the answer in response to question 10 a.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple3ExplanatoryText: Essential Indicator 1) *Other than permanent workers have been engaged to provide Support and Peripheral Services at PFC Ltd. through the outsourced agency. All compliance, payroll, and related administrative functions pertaining to this staff are managed by the said agency. 2) * PFC considered only permanent employees and workers for the mentioned category. 9) *In Column B, PFC has only considered the Permanent Employees/Workers. 14) This year reflects PFC’s strengthened internal processes and proactive efforts to comprehensively evaluate health, safety, and working conditions in offices. This improvement demonstrates our ongoing commitment to employee well-being and workplace standards.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMeasuresUndertakenByT: We carry out routine audits to make sure value chain participants have deposited and withheld required dues (vendors)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif:
PFC recognises that a diverse range of stakeholders is significantly impacted by its operations. These stakeholders include consumers, regulatory authorities, competitors, business partners, and local communities. The organisation firmly believes that fostering and maintaining strong, enduring relationships with these stakeholders is essential. To achieve this, PFC commits to open communication and authentic engagement, ensuring that all voices are heard. Through meaningful interactions with various stakeholder groups, PFC gains valuable insights into both the direct and indirect consequences of its activities across social, environmental, and economic dimensions. This engagement is crucial as it enables the organisation to operate as a responsible corporate citizen, attuned to the needs and concerns of the community it serves.
PFC employs a proactive approach to assess its corporate strategies and operations, carefully identifying the material challenges it faces while evaluating its services and solutions. This thoughtful interaction not only nurtures a positive external and internal environment but also helps mitigate potential risks to its reputation. The company has meticulously mapped its stakeholders, differentiating between internal and external groups. Internal stakeholders include employees, staff, and management who play a vital role within the organisation. On the external side, PFC’s stakeholders encompass a variety of government bodies and regulatory authorities, including State Governments, the Reserve Bank of India, the Ministry of Corporate Affairs, the Securities and Exchange Board of India, stock exchanges, equity shareholders, bondholders, creditors, bankers, borrowers, and customers from both public and private sectors.
To support these relationships, PFC engages with stakeholders year-round through a diverse array of strategies and communication methods, ensuring that their perspectives and feedback are integrated into the organisation’s decision-making processes. This commitment to dialogue not only enhances trust but also drives PFC towards continued growth and improvement in its operations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideTheProcessesForConsulta: Please refer to the Stakeholder Engagement section in our FY 24-25 ESG Report
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesAsToHowTheIn: Please refer to the Stakeholder Engagement section in our FY 24-25 ESG Report
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesOfEngagement: Please refer to the CSR Report in the Annual Report FY 24-25
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Quarterly Performance Review Meeting Periodic engagement through meeting with administrative representative from MoP Parliamentary Committee MoU (Memorandum of Understanding) reviews
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Hosted the ‘Central Sector Entities Meet 2004’ in Goa bringing together over 20 PSUs to discuss on the future of the energy sector, fostering partnerships and driving innovation for the industry’s growth and development.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Mandatory filings with regulators like RBI, SEBI Meetings & Correspondence on regulatory issues concerning PFC Periodic Inspections
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Submissions of detailed ESG disclosures and Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reports (BRSR), aligned with SEBI regulations Regular enangement with RBI on Project Finance Frameworks and ensuring compliance with RBI’s liquidity risk management norms
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun:  Quarterly financial reports, press release, investor earnings calls and investor presentation  Investor relations information portal on website  Dedicated desk for investor relations Meeting with Institutional investors Annual general meeting Annual reports Stock exchange filings Social
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Hosted Investors Meet 2025 for interactive discussions on financial performance and strategic direction
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: One to one meetings Banker meet Stock exchange filings Non deal credit roadshow Bond Issue prospectus
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc:  PFC conducts regular roadshows domestically and internationnally to engage lenders, ensuring consistent flow of low-cost capital. PFC has also forged green financing partnerships like ATFSG aimed at fostering cooperation and promoting sustainable transition finance across Asian countries.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Vendor Development Program Corporate Website GEM (Government e-Marketplace) Portal Grievance Redressal
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Conducted Vendor Development Programs and used the GeM portal for transparent procurement. Introduced a Vendor Grievance Redressal Portal during Vigilance Awareness Week for timely issue resolution.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Business interactions Borrowers meet Customer feedback Grievance channel Interaction through LE/ LFA and other intermediaries
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Institiutional credit checks and due-dilligence with thrid-party verifications. Customer satisfaction surveys
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun:  Company’s intranet portal  Internal communications- newsletter, mails, surveys  Training and capacity development programmes Annual performance appraisal Townhall session Festive celebration Grievance channel Employees Union/ Association
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Organized town halls, training programs, festive celebrations, and sports tournaments (e.g., via Power Sports Control Board). Launched PEEPAL portal for internal engagement and knowledge sharing; celebrated Foundation Day with cultural events.
 Implemented 9 impactful CSR initiatives around the ‘health and nutrition’ theme.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: Human rights are fundamental principles that the Company deeply upholds. We are committed to defending and promoting these rights, ensuring all our business and employment practices are fair and ethical. Regular assessments are conducted to review the resolution of grievances and complaints from staff, vendors, and clients, promoting transparency and accountability. To effectively manage human rights issues, we have established dedicated committees and implemented relevant policies. The Company has a strict no-tolerance policy for any form of abuse—be it verbal, psychological, sexual, or physical.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons:
The company has established a dedicated Internal Committee tasked with taking all necessary and appropriate measures to safeguard the rights of individuals who have filed complaints or provided evidence and assistance during an Inquiry. This protection is paramount, particularly for those who act in good faith, ensuring that they are not subjected to any form of Retaliatory Conduct for their contributions.
To facilitate this, employees are offered a variety of accessible channels for voicing a diverse array of concerns. These concerns may encompass issues such as discrimination and human rights violations, facilitating comprehensive discussions on sensitive topics. Furthermore, the company promotes a culture of transparency and communication through its open-door policy, which encourages employees to approach management or the POSH Internal Committee, ensuring they feel comfortable escalating their issues without fear of repercussions.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfABusinessProcessBeing: The company didn’t face any situation to modify the business process.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheScopeAndCoverageOf: The company upholds the principle of Human Rights in all its dealings.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple5ExplanatoryText: Essential Indicator 1) *For training purposes, we only considered Permanent Employees/Workers. The responsibility for training Non-Permanent Employees/Workers falls to the vendor. 10) While internal assessments on child labour, forced labour, sexual harassment, discrimination, and wages were conducted last year as part of our HR policies and compliance framework, these were not comprehensively documented or reflected in the previous year’s disclosure. This year, strengthened reporting processes and alignment with our HR policy standards have enabled accurate representation of 100% assessment coverage across all plants and offices, demonstrating our unwavering commitment to ethical labour practices and a respectful workplace.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProjectRelatedToReduc: Please refer to our latest FY 25 ESG Report
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: Please refer to the ESG Section in our latest FY 25 ESG Report.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DisclosureWebLinkOfEntityAtWhi:
To ensure uninterrupted availability of IT operations and services during unforeseen disruptions, PFC has developed a comprehensive business continuity and catastrophe management strategy. This strategy reflects the organization’s proactive approach to safeguarding its operations, ensuring that essential business functions continue seamlessly even in challenging circumstances.
In the event of a disaster, access to critical business applications and IT services will transition to the Disaster Recovery (DR) Site, strategically located within a private cloud infrastructure at the Mumbai Data Center. This facility is managed by a cloud service provider that has received accreditation from the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MEITy), ensuring reliability and compliance with standards.
The Business Continuity Plan (BCP) serves as a crucial internal document outlining the procedures and protocols in place to maintain operations during crises. Due to the sensitive nature of its contents, the BCP is not made available to the public, safeguarding the organization's response strategies and operational integrity.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple6ExplanatoryText: Essential Indicator 3)According to the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) database report dated December 24, 2009, it is stated that the water released is regarded as 80% of the water extracted from the source. Consequently, it is estimated that merely 20% of the total water withdrawal is used. 4) Water discharged is regarded as 80% of the water withdrawn from the source, according to the CPCB database report dated December 24, 2009, and is reported as water sent to third parties. The FY 24-25 data has been aligned using the same methodology. PERFORMANCE EVALUATION OF SEWAGE TREATMENT PLANTS IN INDIA FUNDED UNDER NRCD
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MethodResortedForSuchAdvocacy: Swachhta Pakhwada: Organised a host of cleanliness drives and public awareness programmes in and around the Corporate Office, which align with the National Swachh Bharat Mission to raise awareness about sanitation and hygiene.

Funded the installation of streetlights in various villages, jointly with Municipal Corporations concerned. Through its CSR, PFC supported solar LED street lighting, solar home lighting systems, and rooftop solar power plants in rural/remote areas (including North East, Arunachal Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Jharkhand)

• PFC has been successfully organising State Level & National Level Painting Competitions on Energy Conservation, in collaboration with the Bureau of Energy Efficiency, Ministry of Power, Government of India, for students of 5th to 10th in the Delhi/NCR. This is a massive public outreach programme wherein around 10 lakh students participate every year.
• PFC conducts various training programmes for SC/ST/OBC/Women/EWS youth at regular intervals, which help them secure employment.
• Operationalised the RTI Act, 2005: designated PIO and First Appellate Authority, processed RTI applications and regularly filed online disclosures.
• Through its Vigilance Unit, PFC performed periodic inspections, issued procurement guidelines, investigated complaints, and conducted vendor outreach sessions on integrity in procurement.

• Aligned with UN SDGs and the Government Priority Schemes, PFC’s CSR is centred on environmental sustainability, renewable energy, sanitation, education, health, disability support, and disaster relief • Achievements include: o ~ Solar home lighting systems in remote hilly areas o ~construction of toilets in schools o Holding plantation drives for promoting environment & sustainability
• Sponsoring cochlear implant surgeries for hearing-impaired children, providing aids and appliances (e.g., wheelchairs, hearing aids) for persons with various disabilities.
To promot blended-finance solutions that accelerate climate action in emerging markets and foster public-private partnership s for clean energy and low-carbon infrastructure
To highlight the investment gap in renewable energy and call for competitive financing of storage technologies to ensure continuous, round-the-clock clean power.
To advocate innovative funding models and promote global knowledge exchange, accelerating clean-energy deployment

To share how public-sector enterprises are shifting investment from fossil fuels to renewables, and to emphasise their role in achieving net-zero goals through low-carbon investments and public–private partnerships.
To explain how financing distribution sector reforms under RDSS promotes both financial sustainability and reliable power delivery.
To build technical capacity in smart-grid design, regulatory frameworks, and customer engagement for utility engineers and regulators.

To lead discussions on metering innovations, address challenges in scaling advanced metering infrastructure, and share best practices for using data analytics to reduce technical and commercial losses.
To emphasise the digitalisation of distribution networks—including IoT and AI integration—and the importance of cybersecurity to build a resilient energy system.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: Due to the nature of our business, this has limited applicability. However, we recognise our responsibility to tackle societal challenges. Therefore, we fulfil our Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) through several NGOs and implementation partners. These partners work closely with our dedicated CSR team to implement on-the-ground initiatives, engage with project beneficiaries, gather their feedback, and undertake various other activities.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple8ExplanatoryText: Essential Indicator 1)According to the BRSR Guidance paper, this part covers Social Impact Assessment in accordance with the Right to Fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation, and Resettlement Act of 2013. Given the nature of PFC's business, this is not relevant to the company. 4) Given that PFC is in the financial services sector, the procurement of goods and services mentioned in the report is solely for operational and administrative purposes.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:CSRProject: Project for construction of State of Art Building for Interdisciplinary Centre for Energy Research (ICER), IISc Bengaluru, Karnataka’ through IISc Bengaluru.
Development works in 25 Villages of Bihiya and Jagdishpur Block in Bhojpur District, Bihar inclusive of taxes plus agency charges
Project for Fisheries community development in Machlipatnam
Project for supply, installation and commissioning of 63 nos. of Solar based LED High-Mast Street Lighting Systems in various villages of Azamgarh Parliamentary Constituency, Uttar Pradesh
Project for construction of Auditorium Siddharthnagar through Jila Civil Social Responsibility Association (JCSRA), Siddarthnagar, Uttar Pradesh
Project for Construction of Two classrooms (including supply and installation of sound system, smart board, projector etc.) at School of Legal Studies at Manjeswaram Campus (Kannur University)
Project for Construction of School building and related infrastructure for Adi Udipi School, Udipi, Karnataka
Project for distribution of 2000 nos. of Motorized Tricycle to orthopedically impaired, Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjans) in various locations of 10 States
Project for Upgradation and renovation of school infrastructure in Rama Krishna Mission Ashram, Gwalior, Madhya Pradesh.
Project for upgrading healthcare services at Jayadev Memorial Rashtrotthana Hospital and Research Centre, Bengaluru, Karnataka
Project for establishment of Cath Lab at Swami Vivekanand Charitable Hospital, Dharmawala, Dehradun, Uttarakhand
Project for expansion of Hostel Building in Vanvasi Kalyan Ashram premises, Khanvel, Silvassa, Dadra Nagar Haveli (VKA) by construction of 2nd Floor & 3rd Floor under Second Phase of Project
PM Internship Scheme for 390 candidates (Training 360 candidates + Internship 390 candidates) for one year
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:FromWhichMarginalizedOrVulnera: 4% of all purchases must be made from MSEs that are owned and operated by entrepreneurs belonging to Scheduled Castes (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST). a minimum of 3% of procurement comes from MSEs that are owned by women entrepreneurs.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo:
To provide unwavering reassurance to all its borrowers regarding the company's commitment to fair dealings and transparency, PFC strictly adheres to the Fair Practices Code (FPC) in its lending operations, which is firmly grounded in the principles set forth by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). The vigilant oversight of the FPC is entrusted to the Board of Directors, which conducts regular checks to ensure compliance with these vital guidelines. Notably, during the fiscal year 2024-25, PFC proudly reports that no complaints were lodged in connection with the Fair Practices Code, reflecting a consistent standard of excellence in customer relations. For those interested in reviewing the Fair Practices Code in detail, it can be conveniently accessed at https://pfcindia.com/ensite/DocumentRepository/ckfinder/files/Statutory_Requirements/Codes_and_Policies/Fair_Practices_Code/Fair%20Practice%20Code.pdf
In addition, PFC has implemented a robust feedback system designed to gather valuable insights from its borrowers. Regularly distributed customer feedback forms address key areas such as the suitability of PFC’s products and services, the duration of the loan approval and disbursement process, and the resolution of any customer issues that may arise. Once this feedback is collected and thoroughly analysed, PFC takes appropriate remedial actions to enhance its offerings and ensure customer satisfaction. This proactive approach underscores PFC's dedication to continuous improvement and responsiveness to borrower needs.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfThePolicyOnCyberSecur: PFC has developed a comprehensive structure and detailed policies to enhance its cybersecurity measures. To safeguard its valuable IT assets, the organisation has established an extensive cybersecurity framework characterised by rigorous protocols and practices. Among its key initiatives, PFC has deployed advanced Anti-APT (Advanced Persistent Threat) devices designed to detect and mitigate sophisticated cyber threats. Additionally, the implementation of a Next Generation Firewall adds an extra layer of defence by providing advanced filtering and monitoring capabilities. To further bolster its security posture, PFC has embraced IPv6 technology, which not only enhances connectivity but also improves security features inherent to this protocol. Moreover, the integration of Managed Detection & Response Services ensures that PFC can proactively identify, respond to, and neutralise potential cyber threats in real-time, thus fortifying its overall cyber defence strategy.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: No such instance has occurred during the reporting period.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WeblinkWhereInformationOnProdu: Information about our products and services is available on our corporate website at https://pfcindia.com/ensite. In addition, we also use various digital and social media platforms for disseminating information related to our products and services
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StepsTakenToInformAndEducateCo: Continuous and context-sensitive communication throughout the customer lifecycle has been vital in our efforts to educate and engage both our customers and the wider community. This engagement takes many forms, including well-crafted press releases that keep stakeholders informed, regular interactions that foster relationships, and a comprehensive company website that serves as a hub of information. Additionally, our dynamic social media campaigns reach audiences where they are, while our visually engaging video content effectively captures attention and conveys key messages. We also implement awareness initiatives designed to highlight important issues, complemented by feature-focused audiovisual materials that delve deeper into specific topics. Together, these strategies create a robust framework for raising awareness and enhancing understanding among our audience.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsInPlaceToInformConsu: PFC has a comprehensive disaster management and recovery plan that clearly outlines the procedures to maintain IT operations during disruptions or emergencies. Situated in a seismic zone, the Disaster Recovery facility in Mumbai has been strategically activated. This ensures that, in case of a disaster or cybersecurity incident, PFC can continue its business activities seamlessly.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProductInformationOnT: As a Non-Banking Financial Company (NBFC) dedicated to delivering a range of financial products, the organisation places a strong emphasis on transparency and customer understanding. To ensure that clients are fully informed, the company provides comprehensive disclosures through various channels, including detailed loan agreements, thorough documentation, and accessible information on its corporate website. This commitment to clarity helps customers make informed financial decisions with confidence.
